---
title: Analyzing the RPCA (k.anchors = 20) integration results
date: 2024-06-06
metadata-files: 
  - _metadata.yml
  - ../_quarto.yml
---

# Goal of this analysis

1. Determine the appropriate amount of PC to use in the UMAP and Clustering.
2. Subset the RL lineage subset using PCs for UMAP and clustering
3. Subset the UBCs
4. Run a Jaccard similarity to determine overlap between RPCA and CCA UBC cluster

# Files and directories

## Conda Env

Conda environment `/u/llau/software/mb_scrnaseq/MB_scRNAseq/scrnaseq_Leo/conda/scrnaseq_parallel_env.yaml`

## Input

RPCA (k.anchors = 20) FC integration can be found at `/.mounts/labs/pailab/private/llau/results/integrated/20240605/rpca_20/20240605_rpca_integ.qs`

RPCA (k.anchors = 20) RL lineage can be found at `/.mounts/labs/pailab/private/llau/results/integrated/20240606/FC_integ_analysis/rpca_20/rl.qs`

CCA UBC subset can be found at `/.mounts/labs/pailab/private/llau/results/integrated/20240524/25_pc_without_luo/25_pc_rl.qs`

## Output

Output of this analysis can be found at `/.mounts/labs/pailab/private/llau/results/integrated/20240606/rpca_20_analysis`

RPCA (k.anchors = 20) processed RL lineage can be found at `/.mounts/labs/pailab/private/llau/results/integrated/20240606/rpca_20_analysis/rl/rl.qs`

UBC subset can be found at `/.mounts/labs/pailab/private/llau/results/integrated/20240606/rpca_20_analysis/ubc/ubc.qs`

# Process and Results

Lets load in the necessary libraries
```{r}
library(tidyverse)
library(Seurat)
library(qs)
library(RColorBrewer)
library(patchwork)
```

## RL Subsetting

I've already subsetted the RL lineage in the previous analysis so I will be using that file.
```{r eval=FALSE}
integ_rl <- qread("/.mounts/labs/pailab/private/llau/results/integrated/20240606/FC_integ_analysis/rpca_20/rl.qs")
```

### General Plotting

Lets check how many PCs to use
```{r eval=FALSE}
out_directory <- "/.mounts/labs/pailab/private/llau/results/integrated/20240606/rpca_20_analysis/rl"
elbow_plot <- ElbowPlot(integ_rl, ndims = 50) + theme_classic()
ggsave(filename = "elbow_plot.png", plot = elbow_plot, path = out_directory, 
        width = 10, height = 7.5, units = "in", dpi = 600)
```

![25 PCs seems like a good number to use.](/.mounts/labs/pailab/private/llau/results/integrated/20240606/rpca_20_analysis/rl/elbow_plot.png)

Lets do the other processes using 25 PCs instead. Lets first regenerate some of the plots
```{r eval=FALSE}
setwd("/u/llau/software/mb_scrnaseq/MB_scRNAseq")
resolutions <- c(0.1, 0.2, 0.3, 0.4, 0.6, 0.8)

ndims <- 25
integ_rl <- RunUMAP(integ_rl, reduction = "pca", dims = 1:ndims)

# Retrieve UMAP coordinates
umap_coords <- Embeddings(integ_rl, "umap")

# Calculate minimum and maximum values for each dimension
min_umap <- apply(umap_coords, 2, min) # Min (x,y)
max_umap <- apply(umap_coords, 2, max) # Max (x,y)

min_x <- ceiling(min_umap[1])
min_y <- ceiling(min_umap[2])
max_x <- ceiling(max_umap[1])
max_y <- ceiling(max_umap[2])

buffer_x <- (max_x - min_x)*0.05
buffer_y <- (max_y - min_y)*0.05

min_x <- min_x - buffer_x
min_y <- min_y - buffer_y
max_x <- max_x + buffer_x
max_y <- max_y + buffer_y

# Determining the number of human and mice cells
num_cells <- table(integ_rl@meta.data$species)
human_cell_count <- num_cells["human"]
mouse_cell_count <- num_cells["mouse"]
human_title <- paste0("Human Cell Types (n = ", human_cell_count, ")")
mouse_title <- paste0("Mouse Cell Types (n = ", mouse_cell_count, ")")

cols <- c(brewer.pal(12,"Paired"), brewer.pal(8, "Dark2"), brewer.pal(8, "Accent"))
names(cols) <- levels(integ_rl$common_cell_name)

# Create Dimplot for common cell types
umap_human_cells <- DimPlot(
  integ_rl,
  cells = rownames(integ_rl@meta.data[integ_rl$species == "human", ]),
  cols = cols,
  reduction = "umap",
  group.by = "common_cell_name",
  label = TRUE,
  repel = TRUE,
  raster = FALSE
) + 
  ggtitle(human_title) + 
  theme(legend.position = "bottom") + 
  guides(colour = guide_legend(ncol = 2, override.aes = list(size = 2))) +
  xlim(min_x, max_x) +       # Set x-axis limits
  ylim(min_y, max_y)         # Set y-axis limits

umap_mouse_cells <- DimPlot(
  integ_rl,
  cells = rownames(integ_rl@meta.data[integ_rl$species == "mouse", ]),
  cols = cols,
  reduction = "umap",
  group.by = "common_cell_name",
  label = TRUE,
  raster = FALSE
) + 
  ggtitle(mouse_title) + 
  theme(legend.position = "bottom") + 
  guides(colour = guide_legend(ncol = 2, override.aes = list(size = 2))) +
  xlim(min_x, max_x) +       # Set x-axis limits
  ylim(min_y, max_y)         # Set y-axis limits

ggsave(
  filename = "umap_common_celltypes.png",
  plot = umap_human_cells + umap_mouse_cells,
  path = out_directory,
  width = 20,
  height = 10,
  units = "in",
  dpi = 600
)
```

![Common cell type UMAP](/.mounts/labs/pailab/private/llau/results/integrated/20240606/rpca_20_analysis/rl/umap_common_celltypes.png)

```{r eval=FALSE}
cols <- brewer.pal(4,"Dark2")

species_cols <- alpha(cols, 0.4)
names(species_cols) <- levels(integ_rl$species)

plt <- DimPlot(integ_rl, reduction = "umap", label = TRUE, repel = TRUE, group.by = "species", cols=species_cols, raster = FALSE)
ggsave(filename = "species_umap.png", plot = plt, path = out_directory, 
            width = 10, height = 10, units = "in", dpi = 600)
```

![Species UMAP](/.mounts/labs/pailab/private/llau/results/integrated/20240606/rpca_20_analysis/rl/species_umap.png)

```{r eval=FALSE}
dataset_cols <- alpha(cols, 0.4)
names(dataset_cols) <- levels(integ_rl$dataset_name)

plt <- DimPlot(integ_rl, reduction = "umap", label = TRUE, repel = TRUE, group.by = "dataset_name", cols=dataset_cols, raster = FALSE)
ggsave(filename = "dataset_umap.png", plot = plt, path = out_directory, 
            width = 15, height = 10, units = "in", dpi = 600)
```

![Dataset UMAP](/.mounts/labs/pailab/private/llau/results/integrated/20240606/rpca_20_analysis/rl/dataset_umap.png)

```{r eval=FALSE}
# Genes of interest
genes <- read.csv("scrnaseq_Leo/utilities/cell_gene_mapping.csv")
genes <- genes$gene

DefaultAssay(integ_rl) <- "RNA"

# Feature plots
for(gene in genes){
  feature_plot <- FeaturePlot(object = integ_rl, features = gene, order = TRUE, min.cutoff = "q10", max.cutoff = "q90", raster = FALSE) +
                  ggtitle(paste("Integrated: ", gene)) +
                  xlim(min_x, max_x) +       # Set x-axis limits
                  ylim(min_y, max_y)         # Set y-axis limits

  feature_plot_human <- FeaturePlot(object = integ_rl, cells = rownames(integ_rl@meta.data[integ_rl$species == "human", ]),  
                        features = gene, order = TRUE, min.cutoff = "q10", max.cutoff = "q90", raster = FALSE) +
                        ggtitle(paste("Human: ", gene)) +
                        xlim(min_x, max_x) +       # Set x-axis limits
                        ylim(min_y, max_y)         # Set y-axis limits

  feature_plot_mouse <- FeaturePlot(object = integ_rl, cells = rownames(integ_rl@meta.data[integ_rl$species == "mouse", ]), 
                        features = gene, order = TRUE, min.cutoff = "q10", max.cutoff = "q90", raster = FALSE) +
                        ggtitle(paste("Mouse: ", gene)) +
                        xlim(min_x, max_x) +       # Set x-axis limits
                        ylim(min_y, max_y)         # Set y-axis limits

  plot <- feature_plot | feature_plot_human | feature_plot_mouse
  out_path <- paste0(out_directory, "/feature_plots")
  feature_plot_name <- paste0(gene, "_feature_plot.png")
  ggsave(filename = feature_plot_name, plot = plot, path = out_path, 
        width =30, height = 10, units = "in", dpi = 600)
}
```

![EOMES Feature Plot](/.mounts/labs/pailab/private/llau/results/integrated/20240606/rpca_20_analysis/rl/feature_plots/EOMES_feature_plot.png)

```{r eval=FALSE}
DefaultAssay(integ_rl) <- "integrated"

# Generate clusters
integ_rl <- FindNeighbors(integ_rl, dims = 1:ndims) 
for (i in resolutions) {
    integ_rl <- FindClusters(integ_rl, resolution = i)
    pattern <- paste0(".*_snn_res.", i)
    matching_column <- grep(pattern, colnames(integ_rl@meta.data), value = TRUE)
    new_name <- paste0("snn_res.", i)
    integ_rl@meta.data[[new_name]] <- integ_rl@meta.data[[matching_column]]
    integ_rl@meta.data[[matching_column]] <- NULL
}

for (i in resolutions) {
    resolution_name <- paste0("snn_res.", i)
    plt <- DimPlot(integ_rl, reduction = "umap", group.by = resolution_name, label = TRUE, label.size = 3, raster = FALSE) + 
      ggtitle(paste("Resolution:", i)) +
      xlim(min_x, max_x) +       # Set x-axis limits
      ylim(min_y, max_y)         # Set y-axis limits
    filename <- paste0(i, "_integ_umap_clusters.png")
    ggsave(filename = filename, plot = plt, path = out_directory, 
            width = 10, height = 10, dpi = 600)
}
```

Based on the common cell type UMAP and the clustering, I believe that 0.2 resolution is the best for our case. Going forward, I will use the 0.2 grouping for plots.

![0.2 Resolution Clustering](/.mounts/labs/pailab/private/llau/results/integrated/20240606/rpca_20_analysis/rl/0.2_integ_umap_clusters.png)

```{r eval=FALSE}
# 0.1 resolution
integ_rl$snn_res.0.2 <- fct_relevel(integ_rl$snn_res.0.2, str_sort(levels(integ_rl$snn_res.0.2), numeric = TRUE))

source("/u/llau/software/mb_scrnaseq/MB_scRNAseq/software/utilities/cluster_barplot.R")
human_age_bar_plot <- cluster_barplot(integ_rl, split.by = "human_age", group.by = "snn_res.0.2")
mouse_age_bar_plot <- cluster_barplot(integ_rl, split.by = "mouse_age", group.by = "snn_res.0.2")
bar_plot_name <- "0.2_age_bar_plot.png"
ggsave(filename = bar_plot_name, plot = human_age_bar_plot + mouse_age_bar_plot, path = out_directory, 
          width = 20, height = 10, units = "in", dpi = 600)

dataset_bar_plot <- cluster_barplot(integ_rl, split.by = "dataset_name", group.by = "snn_res.0.2")
bar_plot_name <- "0.2_dataset_bar_plot.png"
ggsave(filename = bar_plot_name, plot = dataset_bar_plot, path = out_directory, 
          width = 20, height = 10, units = "in", dpi = 600)

cell_type_bar_plot <- cluster_barplot(integ_rl, split.by = "cell_type", group.by = "snn_res.0.2")
bar_plot_name <- "0.2_cell_type_bar_plot.png"
ggsave(filename = bar_plot_name, plot = cell_type_bar_plot, path = out_directory, 
          width = 20, height = 10, units = "in", dpi = 600)

common_cell_type_bar_plot <- cluster_barplot(integ_rl, split.by = "common_cell_name", group.by = "snn_res.0.2")
bar_plot_name <- "0.2_common_cell_name_bar_plot.png"
ggsave(filename = bar_plot_name, plot = common_cell_type_bar_plot, path = out_directory, 
          width = 20, height = 10, units = "in", dpi = 600)

species_bar_plot <- cluster_barplot(integ_rl, split.by = "species", group.by = "snn_res.0.2")
bar_plot_name <- "0.2_species_bar_plot.png"
ggsave(filename = bar_plot_name, plot = species_bar_plot, path = out_directory, 
          width = 20, height = 10, units = "in", dpi = 600)
```

![Common cell name bar plot](/.mounts/labs/pailab/private/llau/results/integrated/20240606/rpca_20_analysis/rl/0.2_dataset_bar_plot.png)

```{r eval=FALSE}
# Dot plot
DefaultAssay(integ_rl) <- "RNA"

dot_plot <- DotPlot(object = integ_rl, features = genes, group.by = "snn_res.0.2") + theme_classic()
dot_plot_name <- "0.2_dot_plot.png"
ggsave(filename = dot_plot_name, plot = dot_plot, path = out_directory, 
          width = 30, height = 10, units = "in", dpi = 600)
```

![Dot plot](/.mounts/labs/pailab/private/llau/results/integrated/20240606/rpca_20_analysis/rl/0.2_dot_plot.png)

### RL-SVZ and RL-VZ check
Lets load the VZ and SVZ cells
```{r eval=FALSE}
vz <- read.csv("/.mounts/labs/pailab/private/llau/results/integrated/20240509/cca/vz_cells.csv")
svz <- read.csv("/.mounts/labs/pailab/private/llau/results/integrated/20240509/cca/svz_cells.csv")
```

Now with the cell ids, we can make a DimPlot highlighting the SVZ and VZ cells.
```{r eval=FALSE}
highlight_cells <- list(svz = svz$x, vz = vz$x)
highlight_colours <- c("blue", "green")

# SVZ DimPlot
svz_plt <- DimPlot(integ_rl, reduction = "umap", group.by = "snn_res.0.2", cells.highlight = highlight_cells, 
                  cols.highlight = highlight_colours, label = TRUE, label.size = 3, raster = FALSE) + 
            ggtitle("SVZ and VZ Cells")
filename <- "SVZ_VZ_umap.png"
ggsave(filename = filename, plot = svz_plt, path = out_directory, 
        width = 10, height = 10, dpi = 600)
```

Here are the results:

![The VZ and SVZ are clustering closely](/.mounts/labs/pailab/private/llau/results/integrated/20240606/rpca_20_analysis/rl/SVZ_VZ_umap.png)

Looking at the actual numbers:
```{r eval=FALSE}
svz_metadata <- integ_rl@meta.data[highlight_cells$svz, , drop = FALSE]
svz_counts <- table(svz_metadata$snn_res.0.2)

vz_metadata <- integ_rl@meta.data[highlight_cells$vz, , drop = FALSE]
vz_counts <- table(vz_metadata$snn_res.0.2)

all_counts <- data.frame(
  snn_res_0.2 = rep(names(svz_counts), 2),
  Count = c(as.vector(svz_counts), as.vector(vz_counts)),
  Cluster = rep(c("SVZ", "VZ"), each = length(svz_counts))
)

all_counts$snn_res_0.2 <- factor(all_counts$snn_res_0.2, levels = unique(all_counts$snn_res_0.2))

cluster_barplot <- ggplot(all_counts, aes(x = snn_res_0.2, y = Count, fill = Cluster)) +
  geom_bar(stat = "identity", position = "stack", color = "black") +
  scale_y_continuous(expand = expansion(mult = c(0, .1))) +
  geom_text(aes(label = Count), position = position_stack(vjust = 0.5), size = 2) +
  labs(title = "Cluster Counts", x = "snn_res_0.2", y = "Count") +
  theme_classic()

bar_plot_name <- "0.2_svz_vz_clusters_bar_plot.png"
ggsave(filename = bar_plot_name, plot = cluster_barplot, path = out_directory,
       width = 20, height = 10, units = "in", dpi = 600)
```

![The VZ appear to mostly cluster in Cluster 1, SVZ is a bit more spread out but is more in Cluster 1 as well with a few in Cluster 3](/.mounts/labs/pailab/private/llau/results/integrated/20240606/rpca_20_analysis/rl/0.2_svz_vz_clusters_bar_plot.png)

### Original UBC Check
Now lets locate where the previously identified UBCs are in this clustering
```{r eval=FALSE}
# Loading in the UBC cells
cluster_7 <- read.csv("/.mounts/labs/pailab/private/llau/results/integrated/20240516/fc_subset_analysis/cluster_7_cells.csv")

cluster_19 <- read.csv("/.mounts/labs/pailab/private/llau/results/integrated/20240516/fc_subset_analysis/cluster_19_cells.csv")

cluster_20 <- read.csv("/.mounts/labs/pailab/private/llau/results/integrated/20240516/fc_subset_analysis/cluster_20_cells.csv")

highlight_human_ubc_cells <- list(cluster_19 = cluster_19$x, cluster_20 = cluster_20$x)
highlight_colours <- c("blue", "green", "red")

# UBC Cluster 19, 20 DimPlot
ubc_plt <- DimPlot(integ_rl, reduction = "umap", group.by = "snn_res.0.2", cells.highlight = highlight_human_ubc_cells, 
                  cols.highlight = highlight_colours, label = TRUE, label.size = 3, raster = FALSE) + 
                  ggtitle("Cluster 19 and 20 Cells") +
                  xlim(min_x, max_x) +       # Set x-axis limits
                  ylim(min_y, max_y)         # Set y-axis limits
filename <- "0.2_integ_cluster_19_20_cells.png"
ggsave(filename = filename, plot = ubc_plt, path = out_directory, 
        width = 10, height = 10, dpi = 600)

highlight_all_human_ubc_cells <- list(cluster_19 = cluster_19$x, cluster_20 = cluster_20$x, cluster_7 = cluster_7$x)
highlight_colours <- c("red", "blue", "green")

# UBC Cluster 7, 19, 20 DimPlot
ubc_plt <- DimPlot(integ_rl, reduction = "umap", group.by = "snn_res.0.2", cells.highlight = highlight_all_human_ubc_cells, 
                  cols.highlight = highlight_colours, label = TRUE, label.size = 3, raster = FALSE) + 
                  ggtitle("Cluster 7, 19 and 20 Cells") +
                  xlim(min_x, max_x) +       # Set x-axis limits
                  ylim(min_y, max_y)         # Set y-axis limits
filename <- "0.2_integ_cluster_7_19_20_cells.png"
ggsave(filename = filename, plot = ubc_plt, path = out_directory, 
        width = 10, height = 10, dpi = 600)

highlight_colours <- "red"

# UBC Cluster 7 DimPlot
ubc_plt <- DimPlot(integ_rl, reduction = "umap", group.by = "snn_res.0.2", cells.highlight = highlight_all_human_ubc_cells$cluster_7, 
                  cols.highlight = highlight_colours, label = TRUE, label.size = 3, raster = FALSE) + 
                  ggtitle("Cluster 7") +
                  xlim(min_x, max_x) +       # Set x-axis limits
                  ylim(min_y, max_y)         # Set y-axis limits
filename <- "0.2_integ_cluster_7_cells.png"
ggsave(filename = filename, plot = ubc_plt, path = out_directory, 
        width = 10, height = 10, dpi = 600)
```

Here are the results:

![Cells from Clusters 19 and 20 (human-enriched) from Ian's original integration](/.mounts/labs/pailab/private/llau/results/integrated/20240606/rpca_20_analysis/rl/0.2_integ_cluster_19_20_cells.png)

![The cells from Cluster 7 (homologous) from Ian's original integration](/.mounts/labs/pailab/private/llau/results/integrated/20240606/rpca_20_analysis/rl/0.2_integ_cluster_7_cells.png)

![Original UBC cells of Ian's initial integration](/.mounts/labs/pailab/private/llau/results/integrated/20240606/rpca_20_analysis/rl/0.2_integ_cluster_7_19_20_cells.png)

Looking at the actual numbers at 0.2 resolution
```{r eval=FALSE}
cluster_7_metadata <- integ_rl@meta.data[highlight_all_human_ubc_cells$cluster_7, , drop = FALSE]
cluster_7_counts <- table(cluster_7_metadata$snn_res.0.2)

cluster_19_metadata <- integ_rl@meta.data[highlight_all_human_ubc_cells$cluster_19, , drop = FALSE]
cluster_19_counts <- table(cluster_19_metadata$snn_res.0.2)

cluster_20_metadata <- integ_rl@meta.data[highlight_all_human_ubc_cells$cluster_20, , drop = FALSE]
cluster_20_counts <- table(cluster_20_metadata$snn_res.0.2)

all_counts <- data.frame(
  snn_res_0.2 = rep(names(cluster_7_counts), 3),
  Count = c(as.vector(cluster_7_counts), as.vector(cluster_19_counts), as.vector(cluster_20_counts)),
  Cluster = rep(c("Cluster 7", "Cluster 19", "Cluster 20"), each = length(cluster_7_counts))
)
all_counts$snn_res_0.2 <- factor(all_counts$snn_res_0.2, levels = unique(all_counts$snn_res_0.2))

cluster_barplot <- ggplot(all_counts, aes(x = snn_res_0.2, y = Count, fill = Cluster)) +
  geom_bar(stat = "identity", position = "stack", color = "black") +
  scale_y_continuous(expand = expansion(mult = c(0, .1))) +
  geom_text(aes(label = Count), position = position_stack(vjust = 0.5), size = 3) +
  labs(title = "Cluster Counts", x = "snn_res_0.2", y = "Count") +
  theme_classic()

bar_plot_name <- "0.2_original_clusters_bar_plot.png"
ggsave(filename = bar_plot_name, plot = cluster_barplot, path = out_directory,
       width = 20, height = 10, units = "in", dpi = 600)
```

![The majority of the original UBCs are within the UBC cluster which is to be expected.](/.mounts/labs/pailab/private/llau/results/integrated/20240606/rpca_20_analysis/rl/0.2_original_clusters_bar_plot.png)

### Cell Age UMAP Projection
Lets take a look at the UMAP projections in term of age.
```{r eval=FALSE}
num_cells <- table(integ_rl@meta.data$species)
human_cell_count <- num_cells["human"]
mouse_cell_count <- num_cells["mouse"]

human_title <- paste0("Human Cell Ages (n = ", human_cell_count, ")")
mouse_title <- paste0("Mouse Cell Ages (n = ", mouse_cell_count, ")")

num_bins_human <- 8
colour_palette_human <- scales::viridis_pal(option = "magma")(num_bins_human)

num_bins_mouse <- 17
colour_palette_mouse <- scales::viridis_pal(option = "magma")(num_bins_mouse)

umap_human_cells <- DimPlot(
    integ_rl,
    cells = rownames(integ_rl@meta.data[integ_rl$species == "human", ]),
    cols = colour_palette_human,
    reduction = "umap",
    group.by = "human_age",
    label = TRUE,
    repel = TRUE,
    raster = FALSE,
    pt.size = 0.5
) + 
    ggtitle(human_title) + 
    theme(legend.position = "bottom") + 
    guides(colour = guide_legend(ncol = 2, override.aes = list(size = 2))) +
    xlim(min_x, max_x) +       # Set x-axis limits
    ylim(min_y, max_y)         # Set y-axis limits

umap_mouse_cells <- DimPlot(
    integ_rl,
    cells = rownames(integ_rl@meta.data[integ_rl$species == "mouse", ]),
    cols = colour_palette_mouse,
    reduction = "umap",
    group.by = "mouse_age",
    label = TRUE,
    repel = TRUE,
    raster = FALSE,
    pt.size = 0.5
  ) + 
    ggtitle(mouse_title) + 
    theme(legend.position = "bottom") + 
    guides(colour = guide_legend(ncol = 2, override.aes = list(size = 2))) +
    xlim(min_x, max_x) +       # Set x-axis limits
    ylim(min_y, max_y)         # Set y-axis limits

  ggsave(
    filename = "ages_colour_scale_umap.png",
    plot = umap_human_cells + umap_mouse_cells,
    path = out_directory,
    width = 20,
    height = 15,
    units = "in",
    dpi = 600
  )
```

Here are the results:

![The cluster seems to have an age gradient.](/.mounts/labs/pailab/private/llau/results/integrated/20240606/rpca_20_analysis/rl/ages_colour_scale_umap.png)

Now lets save the object and do a differential gene analysis for each cluster.
```{r eval=FALSE}
qsave(integ_rl, "/.mounts/labs/pailab/private/llau/results/integrated/20240606/rpca_20_analysis/rl/rl.qs")
```

Now we need to switch conda environments and load in new libraries

### Clustree
Lets import clustree
```{r eval=FALSE}
library(tidyverse)
library(Seurat)
library(qs)
library(clustree)
```
```{r eval=FALSE}
integ_rl <- qread("/.mounts/labs/pailab/private/llau/results/integrated/20240606/rpca_20_analysis/rl/rl.qs")
out_directory <- "/.mounts/labs/pailab/private/llau/results/integrated/20240606/rpca_20_analysis/rl"
tree <- clustree(integ_rl, prefix = "snn_res.")
tree_plot_name <- "tree_plot.png"
ggsave(filename = tree_plot_name, plot = tree, path = out_directory, 
        width = 15, height = 25, units = "in", dpi = 600)
```

![Clustree analysis](/.mounts/labs/pailab/private/llau/results/integrated/20240606/rpca_20_analysis/rl/tree_plot.png)

### Cluster Markers

Lets identify the markers of each 0.2 cluster and see where our cell type markers overlap.
```{r eval=FALSE}
library(presto)
```
```{r eval=FALSE}
ortho_csv <- read.csv("/u/llau/software/mb_scrnaseq/MB_scRNAseq/scrnaseq_Leo/utilities/20240402_hgnc_mgi_orth_genes.csv")

ortho_integ <- subset(integ_rl, features = ortho_csv$HGNC.symbol)

ortho_integ$snn_res.0.2 <- factor(ortho_integ$snn_res.0.2, levels = unique(ortho_integ$snn_res.0.2))

ortho_markers <- wilcoxauc(
  ortho_integ,
  group_by = "snn_res.0.2",
  assay = "data",
  seurat_assay = "RNA"
)
write.csv(ortho_markers, file = paste0(out_directory, "/ortho_markers.csv"))

top_markers <- top_markers(ortho_markers, n = 3000)
write.csv(top_markers, file = paste0(out_directory, "/top_ortho_markers.csv"))
```

Now lets determine where our cell type markers rank for each cluster.
```{r eval=FALSE}
cell_markers <- read.csv("/u/llau/software/mb_scrnaseq/MB_scRNAseq/scrnaseq_Leo/utilities/cell_gene_mapping.csv")
ortho_markers <- read.csv(paste0(out_directory, "/ortho_markers.csv"))
top_markers <- read.csv(paste0(out_directory, "/top_ortho_markers.csv"))

# Pivoting and filtering the dataframe
top_markers_filt <- top_markers %>%
          select(-X) %>%
          rename_with(~paste0('', gsub('X', '', .x)), starts_with('X')) %>%
          pivot_longer(cols = -rank, 
              names_to = 'cluster', 
              values_to = 'gene') %>%
          filter(gene %in% cell_markers$gene) %>%
          mutate(cluster = as.integer(cluster))

# Renaming column to be the same
ortho_markers_filt <- ortho_markers %>%
          select(-X) %>%
          rename(gene = feature, cluster = group)

results_df <- left_join(top_markers_filt, ortho_markers_filt, by = c("gene", "cluster")) %>%
          group_by(cluster) %>%
          arrange(cluster)

write.csv(results_df, file = paste0(out_directory, "/marker_overlap.csv"))
```

Based on the cell marker rankings, lets assign a cell type to each cluster.

| Cluster Number | Annotated Cell Type | Comments | Cell Count |
|----------|----------|----------|----------|
| 0 | GC | Top marker is RELN (Aldinger) and RBFOX3 (Aldinger) which are GC markers | 19145 |
| 1 | GCP | Higher expression in TOP2A (Sepp), ATOH1 (Sepp), MKI67 (Aldinger), DCC (Consalez), NFIA (Fraser) which are GCP markers| 10436 |
| 2 | GCP | Higher expression in TOP2A (Sepp), ATOH1 (Sepp), and MKI67 (Aldinger) which are GCP markers| 10111 |
| 3 | UBC | Top markers are LMX1A, OTX2, and EOMES which are the classic UBC markers | 9581 |
| 4 | GC | Top marker is NHLH1 which is a GC marker | 8894 |
| 5 | GC | Top markers is  RBFOX3 (Aldinger) which is a GC markers | 5772 |
| 6 | GC | Top markers are GABRA6 (Sepp) (GC Defined) and RBFOX3 (Aldinger) which are GC markers | 4637 |
| 7 | GCP | Higher expression in ATOH1 (Sepp), and YBX1 (Kwon) which are GCP markers| 4009 |
| 8 | Oligodendrocytes/OPC | Top markers are TNR (Sepp) and PDGFRA (Sepp) which are OPC markers | 3130 |
| 9 | RL | SOX11 and HNRNPH1 are the highly expressed. Also MEIS2 (Aldinger), and YBX1 (Kwon). MEIS2 is a excitatory nuclei marker and YBX1 indicates something a little bit earlier| 1839 |
| 10 | Endothelial | Top markers are CLDN5 (Aldinger) and ITM2A (Aldinger) which are endothelial markers | 1646 |
| 11 | Microglia | Top markers are TREM2 (Yang) and CX3CR1 (Aldinger) which are microglia markers | 1500 |
| 12 | Oligodendrocytes/OPC | Top marker is MAG (Sepp) which is oligodendrocyte marker | 445 |
| 13 | GCP? | Top markers are ZIC1 and NFIA (Fraser) | 295 |
| 14 | Unknown | No marker hits | 103 |

```{r eval=FALSE}
print(sessionInfo())

 [1] LC_CTYPE=en_US.UTF-8       LC_NUMERIC=C              
 [3] LC_TIME=en_US.UTF-8        LC_COLLATE=en_US.UTF-8

other attached packages:
 [1] presto_1.0.0       data.table_1.15.2  Rcpp_1.0.12        clustree_0.5.1    
 [5] ggraph_2.1.0       qs_0.25.7          SeuratObject_5.0.1 Seurat_4.3.0.1    
 [9] lubridate_1.9.3    forcats_1.0.0      stringr_1.5.1      dplyr_1.1.4       
[13] purrr_1.0.2        readr_2.1.5        tidyr_1.3.1        tibble_3.2.1      
[17] ggplot2_3.5.1      tidyverse_2.0.0   

loaded via a namespace (and not attached):
  [1] RColorBrewer_1.1-3     jsonlite_1.8.8         magrittr_2.0.3        
  [4] spatstat.utils_3.0-4   farver_2.1.1           ragg_1.3.0            
  [7] vctrs_0.6.5            ROCR_1.0-11            spatstat.explore_3.2-6
 [10] htmltools_0.5.8.1      sctransform_0.4.1      parallelly_1.37.1     
 [13] KernSmooth_2.23-22     htmlwidgets_1.6.4      ica_1.0-3             
 [16] plyr_1.8.9             plotly_4.10.4          zoo_1.8-12            
 [19] igraph_1.5.0.1         mime_0.12              lifecycle_1.0.4       
 [22] pkgconfig_2.0.3        Matrix_1.6-5           R6_2.5.1              
 [25] fastmap_1.1.1          fitdistrplus_1.1-11    future_1.33.2         
 [28] shiny_1.8.1.1          digest_0.6.35          colorspace_2.1-0      
 [31] patchwork_1.2.0        tensor_1.5             irlba_2.3.5.1         
 [34] textshaping_0.3.7      labeling_0.4.3         progressr_0.14.0      
 [37] fansi_1.0.6            spatstat.sparse_3.0-3  timechange_0.3.0      
 [40] httr_1.4.7             polyclip_1.10-6        abind_1.4-5           
 [43] compiler_4.3.3         withr_3.0.0            backports_1.4.1       
 [46] viridis_0.6.5          ggforce_0.4.2          MASS_7.3-60           
 [49] tools_4.3.3            lmtest_0.9-40          httpuv_1.6.15         
 [52] future.apply_1.11.2    goftest_1.2-3          glue_1.7.0            
 [55] nlme_3.1-164           promises_1.3.0         grid_4.3.3            
 [58] checkmate_2.3.0        Rtsne_0.17             cluster_2.1.6         
 [61] reshape2_1.4.4         generics_0.1.3         gtable_0.3.5          
 [64] spatstat.data_3.0-4    tzdb_0.4.0             RApiSerialize_0.1.2   
 [67] hms_1.1.3              tidygraph_1.3.0        sp_2.1-3              
 [70] stringfish_0.15.7      utf8_1.2.4             spatstat.geom_3.2-9   
 [73] RcppAnnoy_0.0.22       ggrepel_0.9.5          RANN_2.6.1            
 [76] pillar_1.9.0           spam_2.10-0            later_1.3.2           
 [79] splines_4.3.3          tweenr_2.0.3           lattice_0.22-6        
 [82] survival_3.6-4         deldir_2.0-4           tidyselect_1.2.1      
 [85] miniUI_0.1.1.1         pbapply_1.7-2          gridExtra_2.3         
 [88] scattermore_1.2        graphlayouts_1.1.0     matrixStats_1.3.0     
 [91] stringi_1.8.3          lazyeval_0.2.2         codetools_0.2-20      
 [94] cli_3.6.2              uwot_0.1.16            RcppParallel_5.1.6    
 [97] systemfonts_1.0.5      xtable_1.8-4           reticulate_1.36.1     
[100] munsell_0.5.1          globals_0.16.3         spatstat.random_3.2-3 
[103] png_0.1-8              parallel_4.3.3         dotCall64_1.1-1       
[106] listenv_0.9.1          viridisLite_0.4.2      scales_1.3.0          
[109] ggridges_0.5.6         leiden_0.4.3.1         rlang_1.1.3           
[112] cowplot_1.1.3 
```

## UBC Subsetting
Lets import the permutation test library needed for later as well as set the seed
```{r eval=FALSE}
library(scProportionTest)
set.seed(1)
```

Lets load in the RL lineage file. The UBC Cluster is Cluster 3 at 0.2 resolution.

```{r eval=FALSE}
# Subsetting out cells
integ_rl$snn_res.0.2 <- fct_relevel(integ_rl$snn_res.0.2, str_sort(levels(integ_rl$snn_res.0.2), numeric = TRUE))
integ_ubc <- subset(x = integ_rl, subset = snn_res.0.2 == 3)
```

Lets cluster the integration at multiple resolutions
```{r eval=FALSE}
out_directory <- "/.mounts/labs/pailab/private/llau/results/integrated/20240606/rpca_20_analysis/ubc"
DefaultAssay(integ_ubc) <- "integrated"
# Plots
resolutions <- c(0.1, 0.2, 0.3, 0.4, 0.6, 0.8)
# Retrieve UMAP coordinates
umap_coords <- Embeddings(integ_ubc, "umap")

# Calculate minimum and maximum values for each dimension
min_umap <- apply(umap_coords, 2, min) # Min (x,y)
max_umap <- apply(umap_coords, 2, max) # Max (x,y)

min_x <- ceiling(min_umap[1])
min_y <- ceiling(min_umap[2])
max_x <- ceiling(max_umap[1])
max_y <- ceiling(max_umap[2])

buffer_x <- (max_x - min_x)*0.05
buffer_y <- (max_y - min_y)*0.05

min_x <- min_x - buffer_x
min_y <- min_y - buffer_y
max_x <- max_x + buffer_x
max_y <- max_y + buffer_y

integ_ubc <- FindNeighbors(integ_ubc, dims = 1:25) 
for (i in resolutions) {
    integ_ubc <- FindClusters(integ_ubc, resolution = i)
    pattern <- paste0(".*_snn_res.", i)
    matching_column <- grep(pattern, colnames(integ_ubc@meta.data), value = TRUE)
    new_name <- paste0("snn_res.", i)
    integ_ubc@meta.data[[new_name]] <- integ_ubc@meta.data[[matching_column]]
    integ_ubc@meta.data[[matching_column]] <- NULL
}

# Plot multiple cluster resolutions
for (i in resolutions) {
  resolution_name <- paste0("snn_res.", i)
  plt <- DimPlot(integ_ubc, reduction = "umap", group.by = resolution_name, label = TRUE, label.size = 3, raster = FALSE, pt.size = 1) + 
    ggtitle(paste("Resolution:", i)) +
    xlim(min_x, max_x) +       # Set x-axis limits
    ylim(min_y, max_y)         # Set y-axis limits
  filename <- paste0(i, "_cluster_umap.png")
  ggsave(filename = filename, plot = plt, path = out_directory, 
          width = 10, height = 10, dpi = 600)
}

integ_ubc  <- NormalizeData(integ_ubc, assay = "RNA")

qsave(integ_ubc, paste0(out_directory, "/ubc.qs"))
```

### Previous UBCs DimPlot

Now lets locate where the previously identified UBCs are in this clustering
```{r eval=FALSE}
# Loading in the UBC cells
cluster_7 <- read.csv("/.mounts/labs/pailab/private/llau/results/integrated/20240516/fc_subset_analysis/cluster_7_cells.csv")

cluster_19 <- read.csv("/.mounts/labs/pailab/private/llau/results/integrated/20240516/fc_subset_analysis/cluster_19_cells.csv")

cluster_20 <- read.csv("/.mounts/labs/pailab/private/llau/results/integrated/20240516/fc_subset_analysis/cluster_20_cells.csv")

highlight_human_ubc_cells <- list(cluster_19 = cluster_19$x, cluster_20 = cluster_20$x)
highlight_colours <- c("blue", "green", "red")

# UBC Cluster 19, 20 DimPlot
ubc_plt <- DimPlot(integ_ubc, group.by = "species", reduction = "umap", cells.highlight = highlight_human_ubc_cells, 
                  cols.highlight = highlight_colours, label = TRUE, label.size = 3, raster = FALSE) + 
            ggtitle("Cluster 19 and 20 Cells") +
            xlim(min_x, max_x) +       # Set x-axis limits
            ylim(min_y, max_y)         # Set y-axis limits
filename <- "cluster_19_20_cells.png"
ggsave(filename = filename, plot = ubc_plt, path = out_directory, 
    width = 10, height = 10, dpi = 600)

highlight_all_human_ubc_cells <- list(cluster_19 = cluster_19$x, cluster_20 = cluster_20$x, cluster_7 = cluster_7$x)
highlight_colours <- c("red", "blue", "green")

# UBC Cluster 7, 19, 20 DimPlot
ubc_plt <- DimPlot(integ_ubc, group.by = "species", reduction = "umap", cells.highlight = highlight_all_human_ubc_cells, 
                  cols.highlight = highlight_colours, label = TRUE, label.size = 3, raster = FALSE) + 
            ggtitle("Cluster 7, 19 and 20 Cells") +
            xlim(min_x, max_x) +       # Set x-axis limits
            ylim(min_y, max_y)         # Set y-axis limits
filename <- "cluster_7_19_20_cells.png"
ggsave(filename = filename, plot = ubc_plt, path = out_directory, 
        width = 10, height = 10, dpi = 600)

highlight_colours <- "red"

# UBC Cluster 7 DimPlot
ubc_plt <- DimPlot(integ_ubc, group.by = "species", reduction = "umap", cells.highlight = highlight_all_human_ubc_cells$cluster_7, 
                  cols.highlight = highlight_colours, label = TRUE, label.size = 3, raster = FALSE) + 
            ggtitle("Cluster 7") +
            xlim(min_x, max_x) +       # Set x-axis limits
            ylim(min_y, max_y)         # Set y-axis limits
filename <- "cluster_7_cells.png"
ggsave(filename = filename, plot = ubc_plt, path = out_directory, 
        width = 10, height = 10, dpi = 600)
```

![Cells from Clusters 19 and 20 (human-enriched) from Ian's original integration](/.mounts/labs/pailab/private/llau/results/integrated/20240606/rpca_20_analysis/ubc/cluster_19_20_cells.png)

![The cells from Cluster 7 (homologous) from Ian's original integration](/.mounts/labs/pailab/private/llau/results/integrated/20240606/rpca_20_analysis/ubc/cluster_7_cells.png)

![Original UBC cells of Ian's initial integration](/.mounts/labs/pailab/private/llau/results/integrated/20240606/rpca_20_analysis/ubc/cluster_7_19_20_cells.png)

### Age UMAP Projection

Lets take a look at the UMAP projections in term of age.
```{r eval=FALSE}
num_cells <- table(integ_ubc@meta.data$species)
human_cell_count <- num_cells["human"]
mouse_cell_count <- num_cells["mouse"]

human_title <- paste0("Human Cell Ages (n = ", human_cell_count, ")")
mouse_title <- paste0("Mouse Cell Ages (n = ", mouse_cell_count, ")")

num_bins_human <- 8
colour_palette_human <- scales::viridis_pal(option = "magma")(num_bins_human)

num_bins_mouse <- 15
colour_palette_mouse <- scales::viridis_pal(option = "magma")(num_bins_mouse)

umap_human_cells <- DimPlot(
    integ_ubc,
    cells = rownames(integ_ubc@meta.data[integ_ubc$species == "human", ]),
    cols = colour_palette_human,
    reduction = "umap",
    group.by = "human_age",
    label = TRUE,
    repel = TRUE,
    raster = FALSE,
    pt.size = 0.5
) + 
    ggtitle(human_title) + 
    theme(legend.position = "bottom") + 
    guides(colour = guide_legend(ncol = 2, override.aes = list(size = 2))) +
    xlim(min_x, max_x) +       # Set x-axis limits
    ylim(min_y, max_y)         # Set y-axis limits

umap_mouse_cells <- DimPlot(
    integ_ubc,
    cells = rownames(integ_ubc@meta.data[integ_ubc$species == "mouse", ]),
    cols = colour_palette_mouse,
    reduction = "umap",
    group.by = "mouse_age",
    label = TRUE,
    repel = TRUE,
    raster = FALSE,
    pt.size = 0.5
  ) + 
    ggtitle(mouse_title) + 
    theme(legend.position = "bottom") + 
    guides(colour = guide_legend(ncol = 2, override.aes = list(size = 2))) +
    xlim(min_x, max_x) +       # Set x-axis limits
    ylim(min_y, max_y)         # Set y-axis limits

  ggsave(
    filename = "ages_colour_scale_umap.png",
    plot = umap_human_cells + umap_mouse_cells,
    path = out_directory,
    width = 20,
    height = 15,
    units = "in",
    dpi = 600
  )
```

![The UBCs seems to have an age gradient.](/.mounts/labs/pailab/private/llau/results/integrated/20240606/rpca_20_analysis/ubc/ages_colour_scale_umap.png)

Lets continue the analysis for cluster resolutions 0.1, 0.2, and 0.3.

### 0.1 Resolution

![0.1 Resolution Clustering](/.mounts/labs/pailab/private/llau/results/integrated/20240606/rpca_20_analysis/ubc/0.1_cluster_umap.png)

#### Bar plots

Lets take a look at the age, dataset, cell type and species breakdown.
```{r eval=FALSE}
# 0.1 resolution
integ_ubc$snn_res.0.1 <- fct_relevel(integ_ubc$snn_res.0.1, str_sort(levels(integ_ubc$snn_res.0.1), numeric = TRUE))

source("/u/llau/software/mb_scrnaseq/MB_scRNAseq/software/utilities/cluster_barplot.R")
human_age_bar_plot <- cluster_barplot(integ_ubc, split.by = "human_age", group.by = "snn_res.0.1")
mouse_age_bar_plot <- cluster_barplot(integ_ubc, split.by = "mouse_age", group.by = "snn_res.0.1")
bar_plot_name <- "0.1_age_bar_plot.png"
ggsave(filename = bar_plot_name, plot = human_age_bar_plot + mouse_age_bar_plot, path = out_directory, 
          width = 20, height = 10, units = "in", dpi = 600)

dataset_bar_plot <- cluster_barplot(integ_ubc, split.by = "dataset_name", group.by = "snn_res.0.1")
bar_plot_name <- "0.1_dataset_bar_plot.png"
ggsave(filename = bar_plot_name, plot = dataset_bar_plot, path = out_directory, 
          width = 20, height = 10, units = "in", dpi = 600)

cell_type_bar_plot <- cluster_barplot(integ_ubc, split.by = "cell_type", group.by = "snn_res.0.1")
bar_plot_name <- "0.1_cell_type_bar_plot.png"
ggsave(filename = bar_plot_name, plot = cell_type_bar_plot, path = out_directory, 
          width = 20, height = 10, units = "in", dpi = 600)

common_cell_type_bar_plot <- cluster_barplot(integ_ubc, split.by = "common_cell_name", group.by = "snn_res.0.1")
bar_plot_name <- "0.1_common_cell_name_bar_plot.png"
ggsave(filename = bar_plot_name, plot = common_cell_type_bar_plot, path = out_directory, 
          width = 20, height = 10, units = "in", dpi = 600)

species_bar_plot <- cluster_barplot(integ_ubc, split.by = "species", group.by = "snn_res.0.1")
bar_plot_name <- "0.1_species_bar_plot.png"
ggsave(filename = bar_plot_name, plot = species_bar_plot, path = out_directory, 
          width = 20, height = 10, units = "in", dpi = 600)
```

![0.1 Resolution Species Barplot](/.mounts/labs/pailab/private/llau/results/integrated/20240606/rpca_20_analysis/ubc/0.1_species_bar_plot.png)

#### Previous UBCs Bar Plot

Looking at the actual numbers at 0.1 resolution
```{r eval=FALSE}
cluster_7_metadata <- integ_ubc@meta.data[highlight_all_human_ubc_cells$cluster_7, , drop = FALSE]
cluster_7_counts <- table(cluster_7_metadata$snn_res.0.1)

cluster_19_metadata <- integ_ubc@meta.data[highlight_all_human_ubc_cells$cluster_19, , drop = FALSE]
cluster_19_counts <- table(cluster_19_metadata$snn_res.0.1)

cluster_20_metadata <- integ_ubc@meta.data[highlight_all_human_ubc_cells$cluster_20, , drop = FALSE]
cluster_20_counts <- table(cluster_20_metadata$snn_res.0.1)

all_counts <- data.frame(
  snn_res_0.1 = rep(names(cluster_7_counts), 3),
  Count = c(as.vector(cluster_7_counts), as.vector(cluster_19_counts), as.vector(cluster_20_counts)),
  Cluster = rep(c("Cluster 7", "Cluster 19", "Cluster 20"), each = length(cluster_7_counts))
)

cluster_barplot <- ggplot(all_counts, aes(x = snn_res_0.1, y = Count, fill = Cluster)) +
  geom_bar(stat = "identity", position = "stack", color = "black") +
  scale_y_continuous(expand = expansion(mult = c(0, .1))) +
  geom_text(aes(label = Count), position = position_stack(vjust = 0.5), size = 3) +
  labs(title = "Cluster Counts", x = "snn_res_0.1", y = "Count") +
  theme_classic()

bar_plot_name <- "0.1_original_clusters_bar_plot.png"
ggsave(filename = bar_plot_name, plot = cluster_barplot, path = out_directory,
       width = 20, height = 10, units = "in", dpi = 600)
```

![0.1 Resolution of where the original UBCs are clustering](/.mounts/labs/pailab/private/llau/results/integrated/20240606/rpca_20_analysis/ubc/0.1_original_clusters_bar_plot.png)

#### Permutation Test

Lets take a look at the statistically significance of if each cluster species enriched or not
```{r eval=FALSE}
# 0.1 resolution
ubc_test_0.1 <- sc_utils(integ_ubc)
ubc_test_0.1 <- permutation_test(
	ubc_test_0.1, cluster_identity = "snn_res.0.1",
	sample_1 = "mouse", sample_2 = "human",
	sample_identity = "species",
  n_permutations = 10000
)

plt <- permutation_plot(ubc_test_0.1)
filename <- "0.1_permutation_test.png"
ggsave(filename = filename, plot = plt, path = out_directory, width = 10, height = 10, dpi = 600)
ubc_test_0.1@results$permutation
```
```{r eval=FALSE}
   clusters     human     mouse obs_log2FD      pval       FDR boot_mean_log2FD
     <char>     <num>     <num>      <num>     <num>     <num>            <num>
1:        0 0.7138453 0.5945848  0.2637287 9.999e-05 9.999e-05        0.2635984
2:        1 0.2861547 0.4054152 -0.5026045 9.999e-05 9.999e-05       -0.5020010
   boot_CI_2.5 boot_CI_97.5
         <num>        <num>
1:   0.2142634    0.3136006
2:  -0.5864626   -0.4150375
```

![Permutation Test at 0.1 resolution. If a cluster has a positive log fold change past the dotted line, it is more human-specific.](/.mounts/labs/pailab/private/llau/results/integrated/20240606/rpca_20_analysis/ubc/0.1_permutation_test.png)

### 0.2 Resolution

![0.2 Resolution Clustering](/.mounts/labs/pailab/private/llau/results/integrated/20240606/rpca_20_analysis/ubc/0.2_cluster_umap.png)

#### Bar plots

Lets take a look at the age, dataset, cell type and species breakdown.
```{r eval=FALSE}
# 0.2 resolution
integ_ubc$snn_res.0.2 <- fct_relevel(integ_ubc$snn_res.0.2, str_sort(levels(integ_ubc$snn_res.0.2), numeric = TRUE))

source("/u/llau/software/mb_scrnaseq/MB_scRNAseq/software/utilities/cluster_barplot.R")
human_age_bar_plot <- cluster_barplot(integ_ubc, split.by = "human_age", group.by = "snn_res.0.2")
mouse_age_bar_plot <- cluster_barplot(integ_ubc, split.by = "mouse_age", group.by = "snn_res.0.2")
bar_plot_name <- "0.2_age_bar_plot.png"
ggsave(filename = bar_plot_name, plot = human_age_bar_plot + mouse_age_bar_plot, path = out_directory, 
          width = 20, height = 10, units = "in", dpi = 600)

dataset_bar_plot <- cluster_barplot(integ_ubc, split.by = "dataset_name", group.by = "snn_res.0.2")
bar_plot_name <- "0.2_dataset_bar_plot.png"
ggsave(filename = bar_plot_name, plot = dataset_bar_plot, path = out_directory, 
          width = 20, height = 10, units = "in", dpi = 600)

cell_type_bar_plot <- cluster_barplot(integ_ubc, split.by = "cell_type", group.by = "snn_res.0.2")
bar_plot_name <- "0.2_cell_type_bar_plot.png"
ggsave(filename = bar_plot_name, plot = cell_type_bar_plot, path = out_directory, 
          width = 20, height = 10, units = "in", dpi = 600)

common_cell_type_bar_plot <- cluster_barplot(integ_ubc, split.by = "common_cell_name", group.by = "snn_res.0.2")
bar_plot_name <- "0.2_common_cell_name_bar_plot.png"
ggsave(filename = bar_plot_name, plot = common_cell_type_bar_plot, path = out_directory, 
          width = 20, height = 10, units = "in", dpi = 600)

species_bar_plot <- cluster_barplot(integ_ubc, split.by = "species", group.by = "snn_res.0.2")
bar_plot_name <- "0.2_species_bar_plot.png"
ggsave(filename = bar_plot_name, plot = species_bar_plot, path = out_directory, 
          width = 20, height = 10, units = "in", dpi = 600)
```

![0.2 Resolution Species Barplot](/.mounts/labs/pailab/private/llau/results/integrated/20240606/rpca_20_analysis/ubc/0.2_species_bar_plot.png)

#### Previous UBCs Bar Plot

Looking at the actual numbers at 0.2 resolution
```{r eval=FALSE}
cluster_7_metadata <- integ_ubc@meta.data[highlight_all_human_ubc_cells$cluster_7, , drop = FALSE]
cluster_7_counts <- table(cluster_7_metadata$snn_res.0.2)

cluster_19_metadata <- integ_ubc@meta.data[highlight_all_human_ubc_cells$cluster_19, , drop = FALSE]
cluster_19_counts <- table(cluster_19_metadata$snn_res.0.2)

cluster_20_metadata <- integ_ubc@meta.data[highlight_all_human_ubc_cells$cluster_20, , drop = FALSE]
cluster_20_counts <- table(cluster_20_metadata$snn_res.0.2)

all_counts <- data.frame(
  snn_res_0.2 = rep(names(cluster_7_counts), 3),
  Count = c(as.vector(cluster_7_counts), as.vector(cluster_19_counts), as.vector(cluster_20_counts)),
  Cluster = rep(c("Cluster 7", "Cluster 19", "Cluster 20"), each = length(cluster_7_counts))
)

cluster_barplot <- ggplot(all_counts, aes(x = snn_res_0.2, y = Count, fill = Cluster)) +
  geom_bar(stat = "identity", position = "stack", color = "black") +
  scale_y_continuous(expand = expansion(mult = c(0, .1))) +
  geom_text(aes(label = Count), position = position_stack(vjust = 0.5), size = 3) +
  labs(title = "Cluster Counts", x = "snn_res_0.2", y = "Count") +
  theme_classic()

bar_plot_name <- "0.2_original_clusters_bar_plot.png"
ggsave(filename = bar_plot_name, plot = cluster_barplot, path = out_directory,
       width = 20, height = 10, units = "in", dpi = 600)
```

![0.2 Resolution of where the original UBCs are clustering](/.mounts/labs/pailab/private/llau/results/integrated/20240606/rpca_20_analysis/ubc/0.2_original_clusters_bar_plot.png)

#### Permutation Test

```{r eval=FALSE}
# 0.2 resolution
ubc_test_0.2 <- sc_utils(integ_ubc)
ubc_test_0.2 <- permutation_test(
	ubc_test_0.2, cluster_identity = "snn_res.0.2",
	sample_1 = "mouse", sample_2 = "human",
	sample_identity = "species",
  n_permutations = 10000
)

plt <- permutation_plot(ubc_test_0.2)
filename <- "0.2_permutation_test.png"
ggsave(filename = filename, plot = plt, path = out_directory, width = 10, height = 10, dpi = 600)
ubc_test_0.2@results$permutation
```
```{r eval=FALSE}
   clusters      human     mouse  obs_log2FD       pval        FDR
     <char>      <num>     <num>       <num>      <num>      <num>
1:        0 0.42005579 0.3743682  0.16612295 0.00009999 0.00013332
2:        1 0.29041257 0.1978339  0.55381397 0.00009999 0.00013332
3:        2 0.21612098 0.2306859 -0.09409082 0.06279372 0.06279372
4:        3 0.07341066 0.1971119 -1.42495351 0.00009999 0.00013332
   boot_mean_log2FD boot_CI_2.5 boot_CI_97.5
              <num>       <num>        <num>
1:        0.1660783  0.08439546   0.24829314
2:        0.5545135  0.43553673   0.67729933
3:       -0.0933051 -0.21263850   0.02591147
4:       -1.4249384 -1.58833953  -1.26275486
```

![Permutation Test at 0.2 resolution. If a cluster has a positive log fold change past the dotted line, it is more human-specific.](/.mounts/labs/pailab/private/llau/results/integrated/20240606/rpca_20_analysis/ubc/0.2_permutation_test.png)

### 0.3 Resolution

![0.3 Resolution Clustering](/.mounts/labs/pailab/private/llau/results/integrated/20240606/rpca_20_analysis/ubc/0.3_cluster_umap.png)

#### Bar plots

Lets take a look at the age, dataset, cell type and species breakdown.
```{r eval=FALSE}
# 0.3 resolution
integ_ubc$snn_res.0.3 <- fct_relevel(integ_ubc$snn_res.0.3, str_sort(levels(integ_ubc$snn_res.0.3), numeric = TRUE))

source("/u/llau/software/mb_scrnaseq/MB_scRNAseq/software/utilities/cluster_barplot.R")
human_age_bar_plot <- cluster_barplot(integ_ubc, split.by = "human_age", group.by = "snn_res.0.3")
mouse_age_bar_plot <- cluster_barplot(integ_ubc, split.by = "mouse_age", group.by = "snn_res.0.3")
bar_plot_name <- "0.3_age_bar_plot.png"
ggsave(filename = bar_plot_name, plot = human_age_bar_plot + mouse_age_bar_plot, path = out_directory, 
          width = 20, height = 10, units = "in", dpi = 600)

dataset_bar_plot <- cluster_barplot(integ_ubc, split.by = "dataset_name", group.by = "snn_res.0.3")
bar_plot_name <- "0.3_dataset_bar_plot.png"
ggsave(filename = bar_plot_name, plot = dataset_bar_plot, path = out_directory, 
          width = 20, height = 10, units = "in", dpi = 600)

cell_type_bar_plot <- cluster_barplot(integ_ubc, split.by = "cell_type", group.by = "snn_res.0.3")
bar_plot_name <- "0.3_cell_type_bar_plot.png"
ggsave(filename = bar_plot_name, plot = cell_type_bar_plot, path = out_directory, 
          width = 20, height = 10, units = "in", dpi = 600)

common_cell_type_bar_plot <- cluster_barplot(integ_ubc, split.by = "common_cell_name", group.by = "snn_res.0.3")
bar_plot_name <- "0.3_common_cell_name_bar_plot.png"
ggsave(filename = bar_plot_name, plot = common_cell_type_bar_plot, path = out_directory, 
          width = 20, height = 10, units = "in", dpi = 600)

species_bar_plot <- cluster_barplot(integ_ubc, split.by = "species", group.by = "snn_res.0.3")
bar_plot_name <- "0.3_species_bar_plot.png"
ggsave(filename = bar_plot_name, plot = species_bar_plot, path = out_directory, 
          width = 20, height = 10, units = "in", dpi = 600)
```

![0.3 Resolution Species Barplot](/.mounts/labs/pailab/private/llau/results/integrated/20240606/rpca_20_analysis/ubc/0.3_species_bar_plot.png)

#### Previous UBCs Bar Plot

Looking at the actual numbers at 0.3 resolution
```{r eval=FALSE}
cluster_7_metadata <- integ_ubc@meta.data[highlight_all_human_ubc_cells$cluster_7, , drop = FALSE]
cluster_7_counts <- table(cluster_7_metadata$snn_res.0.3)

cluster_19_metadata <- integ_ubc@meta.data[highlight_all_human_ubc_cells$cluster_19, , drop = FALSE]
cluster_19_counts <- table(cluster_19_metadata$snn_res.0.3)

cluster_20_metadata <- integ_ubc@meta.data[highlight_all_human_ubc_cells$cluster_20, , drop = FALSE]
cluster_20_counts <- table(cluster_20_metadata$snn_res.0.3)

all_counts <- data.frame(
  snn_res_0.3 = rep(names(cluster_7_counts), 3),
  Count = c(as.vector(cluster_7_counts), as.vector(cluster_19_counts), as.vector(cluster_20_counts)),
  Cluster = rep(c("Cluster 7", "Cluster 19", "Cluster 20"), each = length(cluster_7_counts))
)

cluster_barplot <- ggplot(all_counts, aes(x = snn_res_0.3, y = Count, fill = Cluster)) +
  geom_bar(stat = "identity", position = "stack", color = "black") +
  scale_y_continuous(expand = expansion(mult = c(0, .1))) +
  geom_text(aes(label = Count), position = position_stack(vjust = 0.5), size = 3) +
  labs(title = "Cluster Counts", x = "snn_res_0.3", y = "Count") +
  theme_classic()

bar_plot_name <- "0.3_original_clusters_bar_plot.png"
ggsave(filename = bar_plot_name, plot = cluster_barplot, path = out_directory,
       width = 20, height = 10, units = "in", dpi = 600)
```

![0.3 Resolution of where the original UBCs are clustering](/.mounts/labs/pailab/private/llau/results/integrated/20240606/rpca_20_analysis/ubc/0.3_original_clusters_bar_plot.png)

#### Permutation Test

```{r eval=FALSE}
# 0.3 resolution
ubc_test_0.3 <- sc_utils(integ_ubc)
ubc_test_0.3 <- permutation_test(
	ubc_test_0.3, cluster_identity = "snn_res.0.3",
	sample_1 = "mouse", sample_2 = "human",
	sample_identity = "species",
  n_permutations = 10000
)

plt <- permutation_plot(ubc_test_0.3)
filename <- "0.3_permutation_test.png"
ggsave(filename = filename, plot = plt, path = out_directory, width = 10, height = 10, dpi = 600)
ubc_test_0.3@results$permutation
```
```{r eval=FALSE}
   clusters     human     mouse obs_log2FD      pval       FDR boot_mean_log2FD
     <char>     <num>     <num>      <num>     <num>     <num>            <num>
1:        0 0.3701365 0.2967509  0.3188052 9.999e-05 9.999e-05        0.3194620
2:        1 0.2592864 0.1819495  0.5110090 9.999e-05 9.999e-05        0.5126692
3:        2 0.1366906 0.2000000 -0.5490855 9.999e-05 9.999e-05       -0.5491553
4:        3 0.1532815 0.1097473  0.4819978 9.999e-05 9.999e-05        0.4818901
5:        4 0.0806049 0.2115523 -1.3920752 9.999e-05 9.999e-05       -1.3929089
   boot_CI_2.5 boot_CI_97.5
         <num>        <num>
1:   0.2280876    0.4126191
2:   0.3868169    0.6431577
3:  -0.6855686   -0.4079395
4:   0.3099684    0.6605994
5:  -1.5487599   -1.2410928
```

![Permutation Test at 0.3 resolution. If a cluster has a positive log fold change past the dotted line, it is more human-specific.](/.mounts/labs/pailab/private/llau/results/integrated/20240606/rpca_20_analysis/ubc/0.3_permutation_test.png)

### 0.4 Resolution

![0.4 Resolution Clustering](/.mounts/labs/pailab/private/llau/results/integrated/20240606/rpca_20_analysis/ubc/0.4_cluster_umap.png)

#### Bar plots

Lets take a look at the age, dataset, cell type and species breakdown.
```{r eval=FALSE}
# 0.4 resolution
integ_ubc$snn_res.0.4 <- fct_relevel(integ_ubc$snn_res.0.4, str_sort(levels(integ_ubc$snn_res.0.4), numeric = TRUE))

source("/u/llau/software/mb_scrnaseq/MB_scRNAseq/software/utilities/cluster_barplot.R")
human_age_bar_plot <- cluster_barplot(integ_ubc, split.by = "human_age", group.by = "snn_res.0.4")
mouse_age_bar_plot <- cluster_barplot(integ_ubc, split.by = "mouse_age", group.by = "snn_res.0.4")
bar_plot_name <- "0.4_age_bar_plot.png"
ggsave(filename = bar_plot_name, plot = human_age_bar_plot + mouse_age_bar_plot, path = out_directory, 
          width = 20, height = 10, units = "in", dpi = 600)

dataset_bar_plot <- cluster_barplot(integ_ubc, split.by = "dataset_name", group.by = "snn_res.0.4")
bar_plot_name <- "0.4_dataset_bar_plot.png"
ggsave(filename = bar_plot_name, plot = dataset_bar_plot, path = out_directory, 
          width = 20, height = 10, units = "in", dpi = 600)

cell_type_bar_plot <- cluster_barplot(integ_ubc, split.by = "cell_type", group.by = "snn_res.0.4")
bar_plot_name <- "0.4_cell_type_bar_plot.png"
ggsave(filename = bar_plot_name, plot = cell_type_bar_plot, path = out_directory, 
          width = 20, height = 10, units = "in", dpi = 600)

common_cell_type_bar_plot <- cluster_barplot(integ_ubc, split.by = "common_cell_name", group.by = "snn_res.0.4")
bar_plot_name <- "0.4_common_cell_name_bar_plot.png"
ggsave(filename = bar_plot_name, plot = common_cell_type_bar_plot, path = out_directory, 
          width = 20, height = 10, units = "in", dpi = 600)

species_bar_plot <- cluster_barplot(integ_ubc, split.by = "species", group.by = "snn_res.0.4")
bar_plot_name <- "0.4_species_bar_plot.png"
ggsave(filename = bar_plot_name, plot = species_bar_plot, path = out_directory, 
          width = 20, height = 10, units = "in", dpi = 600)
```

![0.4 Resolution Species Barplot](/.mounts/labs/pailab/private/llau/results/integrated/20240606/rpca_20_analysis/ubc/0.4_species_bar_plot.png)

#### Previous UBCs Bar Plot

Looking at the actual numbers at 0.4 resolution
```{r eval=FALSE}
cluster_7_metadata <- integ_ubc@meta.data[highlight_all_human_ubc_cells$cluster_7, , drop = FALSE]
cluster_7_counts <- table(cluster_7_metadata$snn_res.0.4)

cluster_19_metadata <- integ_ubc@meta.data[highlight_all_human_ubc_cells$cluster_19, , drop = FALSE]
cluster_19_counts <- table(cluster_19_metadata$snn_res.0.4)

cluster_20_metadata <- integ_ubc@meta.data[highlight_all_human_ubc_cells$cluster_20, , drop = FALSE]
cluster_20_counts <- table(cluster_20_metadata$snn_res.0.4)

all_counts <- data.frame(
  snn_res_0.4 = rep(names(cluster_7_counts), 3),
  Count = c(as.vector(cluster_7_counts), as.vector(cluster_19_counts), as.vector(cluster_20_counts)),
  Cluster = rep(c("Cluster 7", "Cluster 19", "Cluster 20"), each = length(cluster_7_counts))
)

cluster_barplot <- ggplot(all_counts, aes(x = snn_res_0.4, y = Count, fill = Cluster)) +
  geom_bar(stat = "identity", position = "stack", color = "black") +
  scale_y_continuous(expand = expansion(mult = c(0, .1))) +
  geom_text(aes(label = Count), position = position_stack(vjust = 0.5), size = 3) +
  labs(title = "Cluster Counts", x = "snn_res_0.4", y = "Count") +
  theme_classic()

bar_plot_name <- "0.4_original_clusters_bar_plot.png"
ggsave(filename = bar_plot_name, plot = cluster_barplot, path = out_directory,
       width = 20, height = 10, units = "in", dpi = 600)
```

![0.4 Resolution of where the original UBCs are clustering](/.mounts/labs/pailab/private/llau/results/integrated/20240606/rpca_20_analysis/ubc/0.4_original_clusters_bar_plot.png)

#### Permutation Test

```{r eval=FALSE}
# 0.4 resolution
ubc_test_0.4 <- sc_utils(integ_ubc)
ubc_test_0.4 <- permutation_test(
	ubc_test_0.4, cluster_identity = "snn_res.0.4",
	sample_1 = "mouse", sample_2 = "human",
	sample_identity = "species",
  n_permutations = 10000
)

plt <- permutation_plot(ubc_test_0.4)
filename <- "0.4_permutation_test.png"
ggsave(filename = filename, plot = plt, path = out_directory, width = 10, height = 10, dpi = 600)
ubc_test_0.4@results$permutation
```
```{r eval=FALSE}
   clusters      human      mouse  obs_log2FD       pval         FDR
     <char>      <num>      <num>       <num>      <num>       <num>
1:        0 0.22683894 0.23610108 -0.05773636 0.17368263 0.173682632
2:        1 0.24122743 0.16028881  0.58972024 0.00009999 0.000119988
3:        2 0.13933343 0.21119134 -0.60000919 0.00009999 0.000119988
4:        3 0.16532080 0.13032491  0.34315543 0.00009999 0.000119988
5:        4 0.16106299 0.07833935  1.03981592 0.00009999 0.000119988
6:        5 0.06621641 0.18375451 -1.47251888 0.00009999 0.000119988
   boot_mean_log2FD boot_CI_2.5 boot_CI_97.5
              <num>       <num>        <num>
1:      -0.05659823  -0.1695474   0.05951432
2:       0.58855278   0.4502438   0.72484100
3:      -0.59928620  -0.7316415  -0.46504009
4:       0.34548687   0.1853654   0.50614223
5:       1.04194265   0.8442767   1.24830138
6:      -1.47249663  -1.6463934  -1.29798066
```

![Permutation Test at 0.4 resolution. If a cluster has a positive log fold change past the dotted line, it is more human-specific.](/.mounts/labs/pailab/private/llau/results/integrated/20240606/rpca_20_analysis/ubc/0.4_permutation_test.png)

### 0.6 Resolution

![0.6 Resolution Clustering](/.mounts/labs/pailab/private/llau/results/integrated/20240606/rpca_20_analysis/ubc/0.6_cluster_umap.png)

#### Bar plots

Lets take a look at the age, dataset, cell type and species breakdown.
```{r eval=FALSE}
# 0.6 resolution
integ_ubc$snn_res.0.6 <- fct_relevel(integ_ubc$snn_res.0.6, str_sort(levels(integ_ubc$snn_res.0.6), numeric = TRUE))

source("/u/llau/software/mb_scrnaseq/MB_scRNAseq/software/utilities/cluster_barplot.R")
human_age_bar_plot <- cluster_barplot(integ_ubc, split.by = "human_age", group.by = "snn_res.0.6")
mouse_age_bar_plot <- cluster_barplot(integ_ubc, split.by = "mouse_age", group.by = "snn_res.0.6")
bar_plot_name <- "0.6_age_bar_plot.png"
ggsave(filename = bar_plot_name, plot = human_age_bar_plot + mouse_age_bar_plot, path = out_directory, 
          width = 20, height = 10, units = "in", dpi = 600)

dataset_bar_plot <- cluster_barplot(integ_ubc, split.by = "dataset_name", group.by = "snn_res.0.6")
bar_plot_name <- "0.6_dataset_bar_plot.png"
ggsave(filename = bar_plot_name, plot = dataset_bar_plot, path = out_directory, 
          width = 20, height = 10, units = "in", dpi = 600)

cell_type_bar_plot <- cluster_barplot(integ_ubc, split.by = "cell_type", group.by = "snn_res.0.6")
bar_plot_name <- "0.6_cell_type_bar_plot.png"
ggsave(filename = bar_plot_name, plot = cell_type_bar_plot, path = out_directory, 
          width = 20, height = 10, units = "in", dpi = 600)

common_cell_type_bar_plot <- cluster_barplot(integ_ubc, split.by = "common_cell_name", group.by = "snn_res.0.6")
bar_plot_name <- "0.6_common_cell_name_bar_plot.png"
ggsave(filename = bar_plot_name, plot = common_cell_type_bar_plot, path = out_directory, 
          width = 20, height = 10, units = "in", dpi = 600)

species_bar_plot <- cluster_barplot(integ_ubc, split.by = "species", group.by = "snn_res.0.6")
bar_plot_name <- "0.6_species_bar_plot.png"
ggsave(filename = bar_plot_name, plot = species_bar_plot, path = out_directory, 
          width = 20, height = 10, units = "in", dpi = 600)
```

![0.6 Resolution Species Barplot](/.mounts/labs/pailab/private/llau/results/integrated/20240606/rpca_20_analysis/ubc/0.6_species_bar_plot.png)

#### Previous UBCs Bar Plot

Looking at the actual numbers at 0.6 resolution
```{r eval=FALSE}
cluster_7_metadata <- integ_ubc@meta.data[highlight_all_human_ubc_cells$cluster_7, , drop = FALSE]
cluster_7_counts <- table(cluster_7_metadata$snn_res.0.6)

cluster_19_metadata <- integ_ubc@meta.data[highlight_all_human_ubc_cells$cluster_19, , drop = FALSE]
cluster_19_counts <- table(cluster_19_metadata$snn_res.0.6)

cluster_20_metadata <- integ_ubc@meta.data[highlight_all_human_ubc_cells$cluster_20, , drop = FALSE]
cluster_20_counts <- table(cluster_20_metadata$snn_res.0.6)

all_counts <- data.frame(
  snn_res_0.6 = rep(names(cluster_7_counts), 3),
  Count = c(as.vector(cluster_7_counts), as.vector(cluster_19_counts), as.vector(cluster_20_counts)),
  Cluster = rep(c("Cluster 7", "Cluster 19", "Cluster 20"), each = length(cluster_7_counts))
)

cluster_barplot <- ggplot(all_counts, aes(x = snn_res_0.6, y = Count, fill = Cluster)) +
  geom_bar(stat = "identity", position = "stack", color = "black") +
  scale_y_continuous(expand = expansion(mult = c(0, .1))) +
  geom_text(aes(label = Count), position = position_stack(vjust = 0.5), size = 3) +
  labs(title = "Cluster Counts", x = "snn_res_0.6", y = "Count") +
  theme_classic()

bar_plot_name <- "0.6_original_clusters_bar_plot.png"
ggsave(filename = bar_plot_name, plot = cluster_barplot, path = out_directory,
       width = 20, height = 10, units = "in", dpi = 600)
```

![0.6 Resolution of where the original UBCs are clustering](/.mounts/labs/pailab/private/llau/results/integrated/20240606/rpca_20_analysis/ubc/0.6_original_clusters_bar_plot.png)

#### Permutation Test

```{r eval=FALSE}
# 0.6 resolution
ubc_test_0.6 <- sc_utils(integ_ubc)
ubc_test_0.6 <- permutation_test(
	ubc_test_0.6, cluster_identity = "snn_res.0.6",
	sample_1 = "mouse", sample_2 = "human",
	sample_identity = "species",
  n_permutations = 10000
)

plt <- permutation_plot(ubc_test_0.6)
filename <- "0.6_permutation_test.png"
ggsave(filename = filename, plot = plt, path = out_directory, width = 10, height = 10, dpi = 600)
ubc_test_0.6@results$permutation

write.csv(
  x = ubc_test_0.6@results$permutation,
  file = file.path(out_directory, "rpca_ubc_proportion_test.csv"),
  row.names = FALSE
)
```
```{r eval=FALSE}
   clusters      human      mouse  obs_log2FD       pval         FDR
     <char>      <num>      <num>       <num>      <num>       <num>
1:        0 0.22816033 0.23682310 -0.05376187 0.18638136 0.186381362
2:        1 0.20143885 0.10288809  0.96926600 0.00009999 0.000116655
3:        2 0.20276024 0.05812274  1.80260007 0.00009999 0.000116655
4:        3 0.13551608 0.20758123 -0.61521196 0.00009999 0.000116655
5:        4 0.07473205 0.17003610 -1.18604206 0.00009999 0.000116655
6:        5 0.11173102 0.07797834  0.51888449 0.00009999 0.000116655
7:        6 0.04566143 0.14657040 -1.68254580 0.00009999 0.000116655
   boot_mean_log2FD boot_CI_2.5 boot_CI_97.5
              <num>       <num>        <num>
1:      -0.05379672  -0.1675640   0.06379695
2:       0.97162032   0.8033391   1.15048093
3:       1.80629605   1.5818461   2.03781249
4:      -0.61449448  -0.7484878  -0.47749589
5:      -1.18657766  -1.3551302  -1.01712850
6:       0.52083039   0.3188186   0.73802613
7:      -1.68388919  -1.8862332  -1.48033040
```

![Permutation Test at 0.6 resolution. If a cluster has a positive log fold change past the dotted line, it is more human-specific.](/.mounts/labs/pailab/private/llau/results/integrated/20240606/rpca_20_analysis/ubc/0.6_permutation_test.png)


### Clustree

Lets switch environments to the `clustree` env and import clustree
```{r eval=FALSE}
library(tidyverse)
library(Seurat)
library(qs)
library(clustree)
```
```{r eval=FALSE}
integ_ubc <- qread("/.mounts/labs/pailab/private/llau/results/integrated/20240606/rpca_20_analysis/ubc/ubc.qs")
out_directory <- "/.mounts/labs/pailab/private/llau/results/integrated/20240606/rpca_20_analysis/ubc"
tree <- clustree(integ_ubc, prefix = "snn_res.")
tree_plot_name <- "tree_plot.png"
ggsave(filename = tree_plot_name, plot = tree, path = out_directory, 
        width = 15, height = 25, units = "in", dpi = 600)
```

![Clustree analysis](/.mounts/labs/pailab/private/llau/results/integrated/20240606/rpca_20_analysis/ubc/tree_plot.png)


## Jaccard Similarity

Lets take a look at how much overlap there is in the UBC cluster between integration methods (CCA and Harmony)
```{r eval=FALSE}
ubc_cca <- qread("/.mounts/labs/pailab/private/llau/results/integrated/20240527/ubc_subset.qs")
ubc_rpca <- qread("/.mounts/labs/pailab/private/llau/results/integrated/20240606/rpca_20_analysis/ubc/ubc.qs")

intersection <- length(intersect(Cells(ubc_cca), Cells(ubc_rpca)))
union <- length(union(Cells(ubc_cca), Cells(ubc_rpca)))

jaccard <- (intersection/union)
```

```{r eval=FALSE}
> intersection
[1] 8975

> length(Cells(ubc_cca))
[1] 9630

> length(Cells(ubc_rpca))
[1] 9581

> jaccard
[1] 0.8768073
```

Lets also take a look at the similarities between the found "human-enriched clusters"
```{r eval=FALSE}
# 0.2 res cluster 2
human_ubc_cca <- subset(x = ubc_cca , subset = snn_res.0.2 == 2)
# 0.4 res cluster 4
human_ubc_rpca <- subset(x = ubc_rpca , subset = snn_res.0.4 == 4)

intersection <- length(intersect(Cells(human_ubc_cca), Cells(human_ubc_rpca )))
union <- length(union(Cells(human_ubc_cca), Cells(human_ubc_rpca )))

jaccard <- (intersection/union)
```
```{r eval=FALSE}
> intersection
[1] 125

> length(Cells(human_ubc_cca))
[1] 1610

> length(Cells(human_ubc_rpca))
[1] 1314

> jaccard
[1] 0.04465881
```

```{r eval=FALSE}
# 0.3 res cluster 1
human_ubc_cca <- subset(x = ubc_cca , subset = snn_res.0.3 == 1)
# 0.6 res cluster 2
human_ubc_rpca <- subset(x = ubc_rpca , subset = snn_res.0.6 == 2)

intersection <- length(intersect(Cells(human_ubc_cca), Cells(human_ubc_rpca)))
union <- length(union(Cells(human_ubc_cca), Cells(human_ubc_rpca )))

jaccard <- (intersection/union)
```
```{r eval=FALSE}
> intersection
[1] 1324

> length(Cells(human_ubc_cca))
[1] 2086

> length(Cells(human_ubc_rpca))
[1] 1542

> jaccard
[1] 0.5746528
```

## Subsetting out Early and Late UBC cells from Hendrikse dataset
For my project I want an idea of where the early UBCs cluster in these integrations as they have high correlation with Group 4 medulloblastoma

```{r eval=FALSE}
hendrikse_srat <- readRDS("/.mounts/labs/pailab/private/llau/data/Aldinger_2021/glutamatergic_dev_Liam.RDS")
early_ubc <- rownames(hendrikse_srat@meta.data[hendrikse_srat@meta.data$new_cell_type == "Early UBCs", ])
late_ubc <- rownames(hendrikse_srat@meta.data[hendrikse_srat@meta.data$new_cell_type == "Late UBCs", ])
write.csv(early_ubc, "/.mounts/labs/pailab/private/llau/results/integrated/20240606/rpca_20_analysis/ubc/early_ubc_cells.csv")
write.csv(late_ubc, "/.mounts/labs/pailab/private/llau/results/integrated/20240606/rpca_20_analysis/ubc/late_ubc_cells.csv")
```

```{r eval=FALSE}
highlight_human_ubc_cells <- list(early_ubc = early_ubc, late_ubc = late_ubc)
highlight_colours <- c("blue", "green")

ubc_plt <- DimPlot(integ_ubc, group.by = "snn_res.0.6", reduction = "umap", cells.highlight = highlight_human_ubc_cells, 
                  cols.highlight = highlight_colours, label = TRUE, label.size = 3, raster = FALSE) + 
            ggtitle("Early and Late UBCs ") +
            xlim(min_x, max_x) +       # Set x-axis limits
            ylim(min_y, max_y)         # Set y-axis limits
filename <- "early_late_ubcs.png"
ggsave(filename = filename, plot = ubc_plt, path = out_directory, 
    width = 10, height = 10, dpi = 600)

ubc_plt <- DimPlot(integ_ubc, group.by = "snn_res.0.6", reduction = "umap", cells.highlight = early_ubc, 
                  cols.highlight = "green", label = TRUE, label.size = 3, raster = FALSE) + 
            ggtitle("Early UBCs ") +
            xlim(min_x, max_x) +       # Set x-axis limits
            ylim(min_y, max_y)         # Set y-axis limits
filename <- "early_ubcs.png"
ggsave(filename = filename, plot = ubc_plt, path = out_directory, 
    width = 10, height = 10, dpi = 600)

ubc_plt <- DimPlot(integ_ubc, group.by = "snn_res.0.6", reduction = "umap", cells.highlight = late_ubc, 
                  cols.highlight = "blue", label = TRUE, label.size = 3, raster = FALSE) + 
            ggtitle("Late UBCs ") +
            xlim(min_x, max_x) +       # Set x-axis limits
            ylim(min_y, max_y)         # Set y-axis limits
filename <- "late_ubcs.png"
ggsave(filename = filename, plot = ubc_plt, path = out_directory, 
    width = 10, height = 10, dpi = 600)
```

```{r}
print(sessionInfo())
```
